PEI Services
Women Of Wellness (WOW) Program
Doula Program
The Public Health Department's Perinatal Equity Initiative (PEI) partners with Roots Community Health to provide Women of Wellness (WoW) and doula services to improve the health of African/African Ancestry women and their babies.
Women of Wellness Program (WoW)
The Women of Wellness program educates, motivates, and supports black women of childbearing age to be healthy and well. The program teaches women how to prepare for a healthy pregnancy and how to stay healthy after birth. Virtual group sessions give WoW clients time to connect, learn, and talk about topics like healthy eating, exercise, managing stress, and more.
WoW clients can also sign up for one-on-one sessions with a Maternal Health Navigator to talk about their personal goals and plan how to reach them.
Sign up for WoW
Doula program
A doula is a nonclinical birth worker trained to provide physical, emotional, and informational support to pregnant people in the prenatal, birth, and postpartum periods.
The PEI program collaborates with Roots Community Health to offer free Afrocentric culturally conscious, doula services for all
eligible pregnant and postpartum women in Santa Clara County, regardless of race or ethnicity.
The Doula Program offers:
Personal support during pregnancy, childbirth, and postpartum.
Assistance with connecting to local resources.
Access to virtual and in person events that focus on wellness, nutrition, and fitness.
Eligible participants for the doula program are:
At least 27 weeks pregnant or up to one year post-partum
Currently living in Santa Clara County
At least 18 years of age
To request doula services, email
[email protected]
.
Get prenatal support
Prenatal support includes:
Child birthing classes that prepare moms and support partner for the birthing experience.
Signs and stages of labor
Pain management techniques
Birthing exercises
Assistance with developing and implementing personal birthing plans
Lactation and breastfeeding education
Emotional support throughout the pregnancy and birthing experience
Get postpartum support
Postpartum support includes:
Assistance with developing and implementing a postpartum plan
Education on newborn care
Swaddling
Infant nutrition
Safe sleep practices
Recognizing infant cues
Lactation and breastfeeding support
Nutrition education and meal assistance
Emotional and mental wellness support throughout the postpartum period
